perturbation.original_text: The Parties hereby form a strategic alliance having the following key elements: (a) Preferred Suppliers. Oxbow and Global Energy hereby designate one another as their preferred suppliers of certain goods and services, as follows: (i) Oxbow shall be the preferred petroleum coke supplier to petroleum coke gasification projects owned or controlled by Global Energy. (ii) Oxbow shall be a preferred supplier of coal, coal fines, gob or waste coal products (collectively, "Coal") to gasification projects owned or controlled by Global Energy. (iii) Global Energy shall be the preferred gasification technology supplier to petroleum coke gasification projects majority owned or controlled by Oxbow. (iv) Global Energy shall be the preferred gasification project operator for petroleum coke gasification projects at sites majority owned or controlled by Oxbow.
perturbation.changed_text: The Parties hereby form a strategic alliance having the following key elements: (a) Preferred Suppliers. Oxbow and Global Energy may, at their discretion, designate one another as suppliers of certain goods and services, as follows: (i) Oxbow shall generally be considered as a petroleum coke supplier to petroleum coke gasification projects owned or controlled by Global Energy. (ii) Oxbow should be considered a supplier of coal, coal fines, gob or waste coal products (collectively, "Coal") to gasification projects owned or controlled by Global Energy. (iii) Global Energy shall aim to be the gasification technology supplier to petroleum coke gasification projects majority owned or controlled by Oxbow. (iv) Global Energy can serve as the gasification project operator for petroleum coke gasification projects at sites majority owned or controlled by Oxbow.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces ambiguity by replacing the mandatory 'shall be' with discretionary language like 'may, at their discretion', 'shall generally be considered as', 'should be considered' and 'can serve as'. This makes the commitment to use each other as preferred suppliers non-binding. This contradicts with the language in Section 7(a) requiring the parties to disclose information relative to the strategic alliance, as the lack of a binding preferred supplier agreement makes the 'strategic alliance' undefined and therefore unclear what information needs to be shared.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (a) Access to Information. Global Energy and Oxbow shall, in good faith, and subject to the terms and conditions hereof, disclose to one another such information relative to the strategic alliance contemplated by this Agreement as may be necessary or appropriate to effectuate the purposes thereof.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces mandatory language ('shall be') with discretionary language ('may', 'should', 'can'), weakening the commitment to a preferred supplier relationship. This introduces ambiguity and makes the 'strategic alliance' undefined. This directly contradicts Section (a), which mandates the disclosure of information 'relative to the strategic alliance contemplated by this Agreement'. If the alliance is no longer clearly defined due to the lack of mandatory preferred supplier terms, it becomes unclear what information is 'necessary or appropriate to effectuate the purposes thereof', creating a strong contradiction and potential for disputes regarding information disclosure obligations.

perturbation.original_text: The obligations of the Parties pursuant to this Section 2 are subject to continued demonstrated performance and their mutual agreement on the schedule, pricing, financing, economics and other terms and conditions applicable to any such project.
perturbation.changed_text: The obligations of the Parties pursuant to this Section 2 are subject to the sole discretion of each party and their assessment of the schedule, pricing, financing, economics and other terms and conditions applicable to any such project.
perturbation.explanation: This change replaces 'mutual agreement' with 'sole discretion of each party'. This allows either party to unilaterally decide whether the obligations in Section 2 are binding, contradicting the overall intent of a 'strategic alliance' and also contradicting with Section 7(d)(i) that mandates the parties to cooperate and obtain necessary approvals for their obligations. Now a party can simply state that an approval is not required in their sole discretion and not cooperate in getting it.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (d) Regulatory Approvals. (i) Each Party shall use all commercially reasonable efforts to obtain all authorizations, consents, orders, and approvals of, and to give all notices to and make all filings with, all Governmental Authorities (including those pertaining to the Governmental Approvals) and third parties that may be or become necessary for its execution and delivery of, and the performance of its obligations under, this Agreement and will cooperate fully with the other Party in promptly seeking to obtain all such authorizations, consents, orders, and approvals, giving such notices, and making such filings.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'mutual agreement' with 'sole discretion of each party' regarding obligations in Section 2. This directly contradicts section 7(d)(i), which mandates cooperation and obtaining necessary approvals for fulfilling obligations. By allowing a party to unilaterally decide on approval requirements, the change directly undermines the cooperation requirement, creating a clear contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Each Party agrees that it will not circumvent or attempt to circumvent the other by contacting or participating with any third party with respect to, or otherwise attempting to consummate, the transactions contemplated by this Agreement, except in participation with each other.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party agrees that it will endeavor to not circumvent or attempt to circumvent the other by contacting or participating with any third party with respect to, or otherwise attempting to consummate, the transactions contemplated by this Agreement, except in participation with each other.
perturbation.explanation: This change weakens the 'Exclusive Dealing' covenant by replacing 'agrees that it will not' with 'agrees that it will endeavor to not'. This creates an ambiguity as to the level of commitment required, making enforcement difficult. This change directly conflicts with Section 10(a) which indicates that 'All contracts contemplated to be entered into by the Parties pursuant to this Agreement shall be negotiated in good faith'. Making the exclusive dealing covenant not binding makes the good faith negotiation useless if a party can circumvent without a real commitement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (a) Contracts. All contracts contemplated to be entered into by the Parties pursuant to this Agreement shall be negotiated in good faith and shall contain terms and conditions, and be performed for prices, which are commercially reasonable.
perturbation.justification: The changed text weakens the original binding obligation ('agrees that it will not') to a weaker commitment ('agrees that it will endeavor to not'). This directly contradicts Section 10(a), which requires good faith negotiation for all contracts. By weakening the exclusivity obligation, the change undermines the 'good faith' requirement, because it allows a party to circumvent the exclusive dealing agreement with a lower standard of commitment, creating a conflict with the explicit expectation of good faith negotiations. This constitutes a strong in-text contradiction.
